Off Calendar Oct-14-2025 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kristina Lam for Administrator Alicia Thompson (via Zoom).

The Court called the matter on the first and final report of the administrator.

The Court inquired about a four-month delay in addressing examiner's notes. Ms. Lam explained that the delay resulted from difficulties reconstructing receipts related to contract work performed following a building inspection violation. The original receipts had been damaged due to water exposure, and efforts were being made to obtain duplicates from the contractors.

The Court noted that eleven outstanding issues remained, including the absence of proposed orders and the required DE-120 notice of hearing. The Court expressed dissatisfaction with the lack of progress and questioned why the petition should remain on calendar. Ms. Lam apologized for the delay and assured the Court that work was ongoing to address the notes.

Following discussion, the Court took the petition off calendar and set a hearing for status on close of estate for October 14, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kristina Lam for Alicia D. Thompson, Administrator (via Zoom).

Ms. Lam reported that the estate is in the process of obtaining a final waiver from one of the heirs and requested additional time (60 days) to complete the process. The court continued the hearing to March 4, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 with the expectation that either a petition for final distribution or a petition under Probate Code section 12200 will be filed before the next hearing.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for Alicia Thompson (administrator).

Mrs. Navia requested 90-days and stated that they have a final report ready to be filed, but they realized that they did not provide notice to the Franchise Tax board and that they need to get a death certificate. Status on close of estate is continued to December 31, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Wong. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Final Accounting And Report
Off Calendar Aug-28-2023 Continued To Nov-20-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-18-302457 Estate of Thelma M. Thompson and
PES-20-303966 Estate of Roosevelt Thompson.

Appearances: Kristina Lam appeared by zoom for Keisha Smith (Thelma's Estate) and for Alicia Thompson (Roosevelt's Estate).

Mrs. Lam stated that they have spoken with the family members and that they indicated that they would not be pursuing independent counsel to investigate potential challenges to the joint tenancy issue. Mrs. Lam also stated that they are working with Keisha to file a substitution of attorney, that Keisha will be in pro per, that they believe Keisha will be withdrawing her petition and that they will be filing the substitution within 30-days. In addition, Mrs. Lam stated that the only asset in Roosevelt's estate is the property, that they are working with the probate referee on finalizing the inventory and appraisal, and that she believes they will be asking for more time in his estate because they want to make sure that the conservatorship case is closed. The Petition for Letters of Administration in Thelma's estate is continued to November 27, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate in Roosevelt's estate is continued to November 20, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Sae Phan. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Mar-07-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia via Zoom, for and with Keisha Smith, via Zoom; Roosevelt Thompson, Jr., interested Party.

The court confirms with Ms. Smith that she does not intend to hire independent counsel and advises of her understanding regarding this and the related matter and what she expects. The court confirms that she is aware of the ramifications of not hiring independent counsel and that the court wanted to confirm that the parties have been properly advised of the preceding. Mr. Thompson, Jr., advises that he does not intend to hire independent counsel. Ms. Navia reports of a meeting amongst family members to discuss the related Estate matters, of their respective positions and of attempting to settle the matters without litigation. Mr. Thompson, Jr., confirms Ms. Navia's report. The court confirms that it is inclined to grant an extension of Administration in this matter. The court confirms with Ms. Navia that she wishes to continue the related matter of the Estate of Thelma Thompson to track this matter.

The matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jan-03-2023 Continued To Feb-14-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kristina Lam, via Zoom, for Alicia D. Thompson, Personal Representative.

The court reminds Ms. Lam that notice of hearing and Inventory and Appraisal (I&A) have yet to be filed. Ms. Lam reports on the delays and status of the preceding and that the plan is to file an accounting as soon as the I&A issue has been resolved.

The subject matter is continued to Feb-14-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The court reminds Ms. Lam that it expects the filing of an I&A and Notice of Hearing, and the submission of a proposed order prior to the next calling.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Appearances: Rowena Navia, via Zoom, for Alicia Thompson, Administrator.

Ms. Navia reports of delays caused by an order in the related Conservatorship matter that required a petition to amend which has been set for hearing on May-25-2022. Ms. Navia provides additional testimony regarding the status of this matter and requests a continuance of three months.

The Status on Close on Estate is continued to Jul-12-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court advises Ms. Navia that it expects either a Petition for Final Distribution or a Petition to Approve Status of Administration filed by the next hearing date.
